rmk:
De Zwarte Kraai voedt twee uitgevlogen jongen. Hij ziet een Havik die op 450 meter afstand in een boom gaat zitten. De kraai begint te roepen en vliegt op de Havik af. Die houdt het voor gezien terwijl de kraai naar hem onderweg is ....


Carrion Crow feeds two fledged young. He sees a Goshawk that perches in a tree 450 meters away. The Crow starts calling and flies towards the Goshawk. The hawk gives up while the crow is on his way to him and leaves the tree ....
Heared this bird a lot of times (all year long), never seen it. Usualy 4 (sometimes 3 or 5) times in a row,.

rmk: automatically recorded at dawn
A group of crows calling from high in the trees.
Loud and clear
Bird seen making this unusual sound.
loud and clear!
Pair in nesting tree. One started calling like this when a Buzzard flew low overhead and continued after that. Recording distance c 8m. [52.101989, 4.338971]

loc: Ambt Delden, Hof van Twente, Overijssel
Krekengebied Noordpolder van Delfgauw., Pijnacker, ZH
rmk: Voices from a changing congregation of up to 18 birds, sitting high up in trees. Most noticable is a rattle-call, which from a distance sounds very similar to the drumming of a Lesser spotted Woodpecker (Dendrocopos minor). [52.023, 4.403]
Calling from top of high tree. With soft sound while inhaling(?)  [52.123, 4.340]
The nearest was calling from a nest site. [52.123, 4.305]
Breeding female soft calling in nest on lamppost, possibly greeting the approaching male. [52.095, 4.262]
Two birds on breakwater. [52.102, 4.253]
Flushed from bush
Conflict between two pairs. Recorded in mono with Sony Cybershot DSC-S600.
less common call type, up in a tree
mobbing a Buzzard
three to four indiv in open woodland
a juv (fully grown) seemingly begging from its parents up in a tree.

rmk: Posted as mystery, possible Carrion Crow first, hence the species name in the filename. The call seems a little on the 'light side' for a Northern Raven. Nocturnal migration. Recorded from backyard. Olympus LS12 with 2 Primo EM172 mics.
